page: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of system memory. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance utilizes Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization. The hardware allocation provides 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring dedicated execution resources. It features 32 KB of L1d cache, 0.25 MB of L2 cache, and 50 MB of L3 cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instruction sets. No GPU accelerators are included, but the instance comes bundled with 82 GB of local storage.

Benchmark evaluations place the A8_v2 in the lower performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the bottom 10%, while multi-core metrics register in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with read-only large block transfers reaching 26.28 GB/sec and write operations at 17.51 GB/sec, both in the bottom 25%. Computational tasks such as software compilation with a Geekbench clang score of 2857.0 and ray tracing at 2997.0 show weak performance, while text processing and image processing fall into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the A8_v2 offers an entry-level hardware profile with dedicated resources, making it a budget-conscious option for workloads that do not require high-throughput memory or rapid CPU execution. Given the weak database operations benchmark of 1757.71 and low static web serving throughput of 2.59 GB/sec, this server is best suited for basic utility workloads, light development or testing environments, and non-critical background processing tasks rather than high-performance database or intensive web serving applications.
description: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 vCPUs running at 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, paired with 16.0 GB of RAM and 82 GB of bundled local storage. Operating on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Benchmark data indicates limited performance, with single-core and multi-core CPU metrics placing in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read speeds of 26.28 GB/sec and write speeds of 17.51 GB/sec. Lacking GPU acceleration, the A8_v2 represents a low-tier resource allocation. It is qualitatively positioned as a budget-friendly option for non-demanding workloads. This server is best suited for basic utility tasks, light development, testing environments, and low-throughput background processes where dedicated vCPUs are required without the need for high computational or memory performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 is an Av2-family virtual machine with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 82 GB local storage, offering entry-level performance for basic utility workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure A8_v2 features 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 82 GB local storage, suited for basic utility and development tasks.
tagline: An entry-level virtual machine with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and bundled local storage for basic utility and development workloads.

16.0 GB of system memory and 82 GB of bundled local storage
Microsoft Hyper-V virtualization on x86_64 architecture
Low-tier benchmark performance across CPU and memory tests
Suited for basic utility workloads, testing, and light development
